name: EVERGREEN CLOTHING
address: 945 NW WALL ST STE 150, BEND, OR 97703

fr.elements: Main Building; Vehicle Parking Area; Landscaped Area
fr.considerations: Limited Rear Access; Single Access Point
fr.recommendations: Enhancing responder access and operational effectiveness on the property could be achieved by optimizing the current access layout. Expanding the existing single access point or implementing additional access points, where feasible, would potentially improve ingress and egress during emergencies. Enhancing the visibility and navigability around the property, especially where access is limited to the rear, may assist responders in executing timed operations more efficiently. Ensuring that vehicle parking areas are logically arranged and unobstructed may enhance movement and staging capabilities, allowing emergency vehicles to park and maneuver as needed. Additionally, managing any visual or physical impediments around the landscaped areas could support clear responder pathways and effective navigation throughout the premises.

wildfire.conditions: Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Scattered Trees; Vegetated Perimeter; Limited Defensible Space
wildfire.recommendations: Improving defensible space around the property by reducing vegetation density adjacent to the structure and managing the scattered trees to maintain adequate separation could enhance wildfire resilience. Creating more open space near the structure can reduce fuel continuity and exposure, especially if flammable vegetation is currently close to the home. Maintaining a clear, accessible perimeter not only aids in reducing wildfire intensity but also supports firefighter access and suppression efforts. Additionally, addressing vegetation concentrations around the perimeter by selectively thinning and removing underbrush may help create effective fuel breaks, thereby mitigating fire spread toward key areas of the property. Strategic vegetation management focused on increasing the separation between combustible elements could significantly strengthen the property's preparedness against wildfires in the long term.
